label: Client pitch and pricing preparation
recoveryFocus: pricing deltas, deck completion, and commercial framing
nextSteps: Re-open the pricing, proposal, and presentation materials with the strongest commercial context first.
Capture the missing pricing deltas and translate them into a single customer-facing summary.
Finish the renamed deck or proposal asset before reopening lower-priority tabs.

label: Authentication or token debugging
recoveryFocus: expiry handling, refresh flows, and session verification
nextSteps: Replay the failing auth path with the same token refresh sequence and compare expiry timestamps.
Inspect the modified auth files and terminal commands to isolate the refresh or session handoff bug.
Patch the narrowest auth boundary first, then rerun the affected login or token tests.

label: Literature review or research synthesis
recoveryFocus: paper comparison, citation completion, and synthesis writing
nextSteps: Group the open papers by method or claim before writing any new prose.
Turn the strongest note fragments into a synthesis paragraph while the comparison context is still fresh.
Finish the missing citations or bibliography entries before reopening unrelated tabs.

label: Release triage or incident response
recoveryFocus: containment, rollback checks, and operational sequencing
nextSteps: Rebuild the incident timeline from the latest commands, branches, and notes before making new changes.
Confirm the rollback or mitigation path against the current branch and modified files.
Write a short containment summary and decide the next operational check.

label: Product planning or roadmap shaping
recoveryFocus: scope decisions, milestone shaping, and backlog movement
nextSteps: Re-open the highest-signal planning notes and identify the unresolved scope decision.
Translate the latest notes into milestone or backlog updates before adding new detail.
Document the core tradeoff in one place so the session can resume cleanly later.

label: Job application packaging
recoveryFocus: resume tailoring, role matching, and submission readiness
nextSteps: Match the latest role requirements against the modified resume or cover letter assets.
Finish the role-specific bullets or paragraphs before switching to lower-priority forms.
Double-check the final attachment names and submission readiness.
